payload.systemPrompt:
# Executor Agent — System Prompt (MVP)

You are **Executor Agent**. Your sole job: convert a user's build request into a **strict JSON object** that lists files to write to disk.

## Absolute Rules
1) **Only output JSON** (no markdown, no code fences, no prose).
2) JSON **must validate** against the provided schema.
3) All file paths must be **POSIX relative** under a project root (no leading slash, no `..`).
4) **Runnable minimalism:** produce the smallest complete project that runs locally.
5) Prefer **Node.js + TypeScript** (when unspecified). Provide a `README.md` with run steps.

## JSON Schema (reference)
- `project_name` (string, optional)
- `files` (array of `{ path, contents }`) — **required**
- `notes` (array of strings, optional)
- `hasTests` (boolean, **required**) — MUST be `true` when you provide test files

## Quality Bar
- Files must be coherent and self-consistent.
- No placeholders like “ADD YOUR KEY HERE.” If a key is needed, instruct to use env var via `.env.example`.
- Avoid binary assets; use text-only examples.
- **Testing is mandatory**: always generate at least one automated test file in `tests/` or `__tests__/`.
- Set `hasTests: true` in the JSON output when you include test files.

## Example intents
- “hello world api” → Produce a minimal Node/TS HTTP server with a `GET /` returning "Hello World".
- “hello world web app” → Produce minimal Node/TS static server + `index.html` with “Hello World”.

### Example Response Outline
{
  "project_name": "awesome-tool",
  "files": [
    { "path": "src/index.ts", "contents": "// implementation" },
    { "path": "tests/index.test.ts", "contents": "// tests" }
  ],
  "notes": ["Tests can be run with npm test"],
  "hasTests": true
}

Return only the JSON object that matches the schema.
payload.effectivePrompt: make a ui with hello wolrd rainbows
payload.originalPrompt: make a ui with hello wolrd rainbows

# Executor Agent — System Prompt (MVP)

You are **Executor Agent**. Your sole job: convert a user's build request into a **strict JSON object** that lists files to write to disk.

## Absolute Rules
1) **Only output JSON** (no markdown, no code fences, no prose).
2) JSON **must validate** against the provided schema.
3) All file paths must be **POSIX relative** under a project root (no leading slash, no `..`).
4) **Runnable minimalism:** produce the smallest complete project that runs locally.
5) Prefer **Node.js + TypeScript** (when unspecified). Provide a `README.md` with run steps.

## JSON Schema (reference)
- `project_name` (string, optional)
- `files` (array of `{ path, contents }`) — **required**
- `notes` (array of strings, optional)
- `hasTests` (boolean, **required**) — MUST be `true` when you provide test files

## Quality Bar
- Files must be coherent and self-consistent.
- No placeholders like “ADD YOUR KEY HERE.” If a key is needed, instruct to use env var via `.env.example`.
- Avoid binary assets; use text-only examples.
- **Testing is mandatory**: always generate at least one automated test file in `tests/` or `__tests__/`.
- Set `hasTests: true` in the JSON output when you include test files.

## Example intents
- “hello world api” → Produce a minimal Node/TS HTTP server with a `GET /` returning "Hello World".
- “hello world web app” → Produce minimal Node/TS static server + `index.html` with “Hello World”.

### Example Response Outline
{
  "project_name": "awesome-tool",
  "files": [
    { "path": "src/index.ts", "contents": "// implementation" },
    { "path": "tests/index.test.ts", "contents": "// tests" }
  ],
  "notes": ["Tests can be run with npm test"],
  "hasTests": true

response.generated: make a ui with hello wolrd rainbows

taskPlan.originalPrompt: make a ui with hello wolrd rainbows

subtasks.title: Define requirements
subtasks.description: Document exact functional and visual requirements for a UI that displays a "Hello World" message with rainbow styling and any animations.
subtasks.successCriteria: A one-page requirements note exists specifying text content, target platforms, colors/animations, and accessibility expectations.

subtasks.title: Create design mockup
subtasks.description: Produce a static mockup (image or HTML prototype) showing layout, typography, and rainbow color treatment for the Hello World UI.
subtasks.successCriteria: Mockup shows final placement of the message, color palette for the rainbow, and a notes section describing animation behavior.

subtasks.title: Set up project scaffold
subtasks.description: Initialize project repository, install tooling (build system, dev server), and create base files (index.html, styles, main JS/TS).
subtasks.successCriteria: Repository with working dev server that serves index.html and a starter CSS/JS bundle is created and runnable locally.

subtasks.title: Implement UI components
subtasks.description: Build the HTML/CSS/JS components that render the Hello World message and support injecting styling classes for rainbow effects.
subtasks.successCriteria: Hello World text renders in the app with correct semantics and is targetable by CSS/JS for styling and animation.

subtasks.title: Add rainbow styling and animation
subtasks.description: Create and apply CSS (or JS-driven) gradients/animations to produce a rainbow effect across the text or background per the design.
subtasks.successCriteria: Rainbow colors are applied and animate smoothly at target framerate; design mockup color stops and timing are matched.

subtasks.title: Test and polish
subtasks.description: Perform cross-browser checks, accessibility audit (contrast/ARIA), responsive adjustments, and fix visual/interaction bugs.
subtasks.successCriteria: All major browsers render correctly, accessibility issues addressed (contrast >= WCAG AA where applicable), and no visual glitches remain.

subtasks.title: Deploy and publish
subtasks.description: Build production assets and deploy the UI to a hosting platform or shareable URL for review.
subtasks.successCriteria: A public URL is available serving the production build and the deployed page matches the tested local version.

taskPlan.decompositionStrategy: Top-down: capture requirements, create visual mockup, scaffold project, implement core UI, apply rainbow styling/animation, then test and deploy.

Version: 0.1.0
Last updated: 2025-10-11
Author: UI Team — Requirements Subtask·
Overview·
This document describes the exact functional and visual requirements for a single-page UI that displays a \"Hello World\" message with rainbow styling and subtle animations. It defines what the user should see and how the interface should behave across target platforms, plus accessibility expectations and acceptance criteria.·
1. Primary text content·
- Visible text: \"Hello World\" displayed prominently and centrally on the page.·
2. Visual styling·
- Large, legible type: the primary message must be easily readable on desktop and mobile.
- Rainbow color treatment: the text should use a rainbow gradient made from a clear palette of colors. The mockup demonstrates the palette and placement.
- Example palette hex values: #FF3B30, #FF9500, #FFCC00, #34C759, #007AFF, #5856D6, #FF2D55.·
3. Animation behavior·
- Subtle, non-distracting animation: a slow, continuous shimmer or gradient shift across the text to provide gentle motion.
- Respect reduced motion preferences:…

This document describes the functional and visual requirements for a single-page UI that displays a \"Hello World\" message with rainbow styling and subtle animations. The intent is to provide a clear mockup and a small runnable scaffold for local development and testing.·
Primary expectations (summary):·
- Visible text must read exactly: \"Hello World\"
- Text should be styled with a rainbow gradient across letters
- A subtle animation (e.g. slow hue shift or gradient movement) should be present
- Accessible: semantic HTML (e.g. <h1>), sufficient contrast for readability, and keyboard focus behavior preserved
- Respect the prefers-reduced-motion user preference (non-essential animations should be reduced or disabled when a user prefers reduced motion)
- The project provides a small dev server for local viewing and an automated test ensuring the mockup contains the required elements·
Example rainbow colors (for clarity): #FF0000 (red), #FF7F00 (orange), #FFFF00 (…
